name: Triple Chocolate Chunk Pecan Pie
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons unsalted butter, cut into 1-inch pieces
1 cup pecans (4 ounces), toasted and chopped into small pieces
2 ounces semisweet chocolate, cut into 1/4-inch pieces
2 ounces milk chocolate, cut into 1/4-inch pieces
2 ounces white chocolate, cut into 1/4-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Adjust oven rack to center position, and heat oven to 275 degrees. Place pie shell in oven if not already warm.
2. Melt butter in a medium heatproof bowl set over a skillet of simmering water (just below boil). Remove the bowl from the skillet and mix in sugar and salt with a wooden spoon until the butter is absorbed. Beat in eggs, then corn syrup and vanilla extract. Return the bowl to hot water; stir until the mixture is shiny and warm to the touch, about 130 degrees. Remove from heat and stir in pecans.
3. Pour the mixture into the warm shell. Scatter chocolate pieces over the pecan mixture. Gently press them into the filling with the back of a wooden spoon.
4. Bake until the center feels set yet soft, like gelatin when gently pressed, 55 to 65 minutes. Transfer pie to a rack; let cool completely, at least 4 hours. Serve pie at room temperature or warm, with lightly sweetened whipped cream or vanilla ice cream.
